title: Basic Examples
code:
Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
Bob --> Alice: Authentication Response

Alice -> Bob: Another authentication Request
Alice <-- Bob: Another authentication Response

title: Declaring participant
code:
participant Participant as Foo
actor       Actor       as Foo1
boundary    Boundary    as Foo2
control     Control     as Foo3
entity      Entity      as Foo4
database    Database    as Foo5
collections Collections as Foo6
queue       Queue       as Foo7
Foo -> Foo1 : To actor 
Foo -> Foo2 : To boundary
Foo -> Foo3 : To control
Foo -> Foo4 : To entity
Foo -> Foo5 : To database
Foo -> Foo6 : To collections
Foo -> Foo7: To queue

title: Declaring participant on multiline
code:
participant Participant [
    =Title
    ----
    ""SubTitle""
]

participant Bob

Participant -> Bob

title: Use non-letters in participants
code:
Alice -> "Bob()" : Hello
"Bob()" -> "This is very\nlong" as Long
' You can also declare:
' "Bob()" -> Long as "This is very\nlong"
Long --> "Bob()" : ok

title: Message to Self
code:
Alice -> Alice: This is a signal to self.
It also demonstrates
multiline 
text

title: Text alignment
code:
skinparam sequenceMessageAlign right
Bob -> Alice : Request
Alice -> Bob : Response

title: Change Actor style
code:
actor Alice
actor Bob
Alice -> Bob : hello
hide footbox

title: Change arrow style
code:
Bob ->x Alice
Bob -> Alice
Bob ->> Alice
Bob -\ Alice
Bob \\- Alice
Bob //-- Alice

Bob ->o Alice
Bob o\\-- Alice

Bob <-> Alice
Bob <->o Alice

title: Change arrow color
code:
Bob -[#red]> Alice : hello
Alice -[#0000FF]->Bob : ok

title: Message sequence numbering
code:
autonumber
Bob -> Alice : Authentication Request
Bob <- Alice : Authentication Response

title: Page Title, Header and Footer
code:
header Page Header
footer Page %page% of %lastpage%

title Example Title

Alice -> Bob : message 1
Alice -> Bob : message 2

title: Splitting diagrams
code:
Alice -> Bob : message 1
Alice -> Bob : message 2

newpage

Alice -> Bob : message 3
Alice -> Bob : message 4

newpage A title for the\nlast page

Alice -> Bob : message 5
Alice -> Bob : message 6

title: Grouping message
code:
Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request

alt successful case

    Bob -> Alice: Authentication Accepted

else some kind of failure

    Bob -> Alice: Authentication Failure
    group My own label
    Alice -> Log : Log attack start
        loop 1000 times
            Alice -> Bob: DNS Attack
        end
    Alice -> Log : Log attack end
    end

else Another type of failure

   Bob -> Alice: Please repeat

end

title: Secondary group label
code:
Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
Bob -> Alice: Authentication Failure
group My own label [My own label 2]
    Alice -> Log : Log attack start
    loop 1000 times
        Alice -> Bob: DNS Attack
    end
    Alice -> Log : Log attack end
end

title: Partition across the entire width
code:
participant a

partition p1
b -> c: msg
c --> b: OK
note right: Some right note
end

partition p2
a -> b: msg
note left: Some left note
end

title: Notes on messages
code:
Alice->Bob : hello
note left: this is a first note

Bob->Alice : ok
note right: this is another note

Bob->Bob : I am thinking
note left
a note
can also be defined
on several lines
end note

title: Some other notes
code:
participant Alice
participant Bob
note left of Alice #aqua
This is displayed
left of Alice.
end note

note right of Alice: This is displayed right of Alice.

note over Alice: This is displayed over Alice.

note over Alice, Bob #FFAAAA: This is displayed\n over Bob and Alice.

note over Bob, Alice
This is yet another
example of
a long note.
end note

title: Changing notes shape [hnote, rnote]
code:
caller -> server : conReq
hnote over caller : idle
caller <- server : conConf
rnote over server
 "r" as rectangle
 "h" as hexagon
endrnote
rnote over server
 this is
 on several
 lines
endrnote
hnote over caller
 this is
 on several
 lines
endhnote

title: Note over all participants [across]
code:
Alice->Bob:m1
Bob->Charlie:m2
note over Alice, Charlie: Old method for note over all part. with:\n ""note over //FirstPart, LastPart//"".
note across: New method with:\n""note across""
Bob->Alice
hnote across:Note across all part.

title: Several notes aligned at the same level [/]
code:
note over Alice : initial state of Alice
note over Bob : initial state of Bob
Bob -> Alice : hello

title: Creole and HTML
code:
participant Alice
participant "The **Famous** Bob" as Bob

Alice -> Bob : hello --there--
... Some ~~long delay~~ ...
Bob -> Alice : ok
note left
  This is **bold**
  This is //italics//
  This is ""monospaced""
  This is --stroked--
  This is __underlined__
  This is ~~waved~~
end note

Alice -> Bob : A //well formatted// message
note right of Alice
 This is <back:cadetblue><size:18>displayed</size></back>
 __left of__ Alice.
end note
note left of Bob
 <u:red>This</u> is <color #118888>displayed</color>
 **<color purple>left of</color> <s:red>Alice</strike> Bob**.
end note
note over Alice, Bob
 <w:#FF33FF>This is hosted</w> by <img:https://plantuml.com/sourceforge.jpg>
end note

title: Divider or separator
code:
== Initialization ==

Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
Bob --> Alice: Authentication Response

== Repetition ==

Alice -> Bob: Another authentication Request
Alice <-- Bob: another authentication Response

title: Reference
code:
participant Alice
actor Bob

ref over Alice, Bob : init

Alice -> Bob : hello

ref over Bob
  This can be on
  several lines
end ref

title: Delay
code:
Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
...
Bob --> Alice: Authentication Response
...5 minutes later...
Bob --> Alice: Good Bye !

title: Text wrapping
code:
skinparam maxMessageSize 50
participant a
participant b
a -> b :this\nis\nmanually\ndone
a -> b :this is a very long message on several words

title: Message text spans beyond the involved participants
code:
ParticipantNumber1 -> ParticipantNumber2 : this is a very long message that is very long
ParticipantNumber1 -> ParticipantNumber1 : this is a very long message that is very long 
ParticipantNumber2 -> ParticipantNumber3 : foo2

title: Space
code:
Alice -> Bob: message 1
Bob --> Alice: ok
|||
Alice -> Bob: message 2
Bob --> Alice: ok
||45||
Alice -> Bob: message 3
Bob --> Alice: ok

title: Lifeline Activation and Destruction
code:
participant User

User -> A: DoWork
activate A

A -> B: << createRequest >>
activate B

B -> C: DoWork
activate C
C --> B: WorkDone
destroy C

B --> A: RequestCreated
deactivate B

A -> User: Done
deactivate A

title: Return
code:
Bob -> Alice : hello
activate Alice
Alice -> Alice : some action
return bye

title: Participant creation
code:
Bob -> Alice : hello

create Other
Alice -> Other : new

create control String
Alice -> String
note right : You can also put notes!

Alice --> Bob : ok

title: Shortcut syntax for activation, deactivation, creation
code:
alice -> bob ++ : hello
bob -> bob ++ : self call
bob -> bib ++  #005500 : hello
bob -> george ** : create
return done
return rc
bob -> george !! : delete
return success

title: Incoming and outgoing messages
code:
[-> A: DoWork

activate A

A -> A: Internal call
activate A

A ->] : << createRequest >>

A<--] : RequestCreated
deactivate A
[<- A: Done
deactivate A

title: Short arrows for incoming and outgoing messages
code:
?-> Alice    : ""?->""
**short** to actor1
[-> Alice    : ""[->""
**from start** to actor1
[-> Bob      : ""[->""
**from start** to actor2
?-> Bob      : ""?->""
**short** to actor2
Alice ->]    : ""->]""
from actor1 **to end**
Alice ->?    : ""->?""
**short** from actor1
Alice -> Bob : ""->"" 
from actor1 to actor2

title: Anchors and Duration
code:
!pragma teoz true

{start} Alice -> Bob : start doing things during duration
Bob -> Max : something
Max -> Bob : something else
{end} Bob -> Alice : finish

{start} <-> {end} : some time

title: Stereotypes and Spots
code:
participant "Famous Bob" as Bob << Generated >>
participant Alice << (C,#ADD1B2) Testable >>

Bob->Alice: First message

title: Position of the stereotypes
code:
skinparam stereotypePosition top

participant A<<st1>>
participant B<<st2>>
A --> B : stereo test

title: More information on titles
code:
title __Simple__ **communication** example

Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
Bob -> Alice: Authentication Response

title: Participants encompass
code:
box "Internal Service" #LightBlue
participant Bob
participant Alice
end box
participant Other

Bob -> Alice : hello
Alice -> Other : hello

title: Removing Foot Boxes
code:
hide footbox
title Foot Box removed

Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
Bob --> Alice: Authentication Response

title: Skinparam
code:
skinparam sequenceArrowThickness 2
skinparam roundcorner 20
skinparam maxmessagesize 60
skinparam sequenceParticipant underline

actor User
participant "First Class" as A
participant "Second Class" as B
participant "Last Class" as C

User -> A: DoWork
activate A

A -> B: Create Request
activate B

B -> C: DoWork
activate C
C --> B: WorkDone
destroy C

B --> A: Request Created
deactivate B

A --> User: Done
deactivate A

title: Changing padding
code:
skinparam ParticipantPadding 20
skinparam BoxPadding 10

box "Foo1"
participant Alice1
participant Alice2
end box
box "Foo2"
participant Bob1
participant Bob2
end box
Alice1 -> Bob1 : hello
Alice1 -> Out : out

title: Appendix: Examples of all arrow type
Specific SkinParameter
code:
Bob -> Alice : hello
...
Alice -> Bob : ok

title: Hide unlinked participant
code:
participant Alice
participant Bob
participant Carol

Alice -> Bob : hello

title: Color a group message
code:
Alice -> Bob: Authentication Request
alt#Gold #LightBlue Successful case
    Bob -> Alice: Authentication Accepted
else #Pink Failure
    Bob -> Alice: Authentication Rejected
end

title: Mainframe
code:
mainframe This is a **mainframe**
Alice->Bob : Hello

title: Slanted or odd arrows
code:
A ->(10) B: text 10
B ->(10) A: text 10

A ->(10) B: text 10
A (10)<- B: text 10

title: Parallel messages (with teoz)
code:
!pragma teoz true
Alice -> Bob : hello
& Bob -> Charlie : hi

title: Style for Solid Lifeline
code:
Alice -> Bob : hello
...
Alice <- Bob : hello
